frame_label: Motherhood as Basis of Respect / Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: People really need to learn to see every woman in public as someone’s daughter, sister, wife or mother first. The day men start thinking ‘what if this was my own family member,’ half these problems will reduce. Respect for women should come naturally because they hold families together.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it frames women’s worth and right to safety through their relationships to male kin rather than as individuals. It uses the classic ‘someone’s daughter/sister/wife/mother’ rhetoric and glorifies women mainly for their family role.
original_frame: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
persona_text: You are Rohit. Age 29. Single. A man in Pune. You are posting anonymously on Reddit in a thread where a woman described being groped on a crowded bus and said she was too scared to say anything. You are writing a comment giving her advice on what she should have done in that moment and what women need to do to protect themselves on public transport and why speaking up in the moment is her responsibility.
